title: calomyrtus excelsa
intro: Calomyrtus excelsa, also known as the 'Tibetan wormwood', is a plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to repel insects and parasites, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Calomyrtus excelsa include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve symptoms of fever and cold, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties. Calomyrtus excelsa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and extracts, which can be taken internally or applied topically for its medicinal benefits.
